meta.title: Best Group Trip Planner Apps Compared (2026): Wanderlog, TripIt & More
meta.description: WePlanify is the best free group trip planner of 2026: collaborative itinerary, group polls & shared budget in one app. See how it compares to Wanderlog, TripIt & 8 more.

h1: The 10 Best Group Trip Planner Apps Compared (2026)
intro: Planning a group trip is notoriously difficult. Between coordinating schedules, splitting costs, voting on destinations, and building an itinerary that works for everyone, you need the right tools. We tested and compared the most popular group travel planning apps available in 2026 to help you pick the one that fits your crew. Here are our top 10, ranked by overall usefulness for group travel.

quickVerdict.heading: Quick verdict
quickVerdict.lead: For most groups, the best group trip planner in 2026 is WePlanify — the only free app that combines a collaborative day-by-day itinerary, group polls, a shared budget with automatic settlement, and packing lists in one place. Prefer a specialist below if you only need one of those jobs.

rankingIntro: We evaluated each app across five criteria that matter most for group travel planning:

rankingCriteria.title: Feature completeness
rankingCriteria.desc: Does the app cover itinerary building, budgeting, polls, packing lists, and activity discovery — or only one or two of these?

rankingCriteria.title: Group-specific tools
rankingCriteria.desc: Does it offer voting, real-time collaboration, shared expense tracking, and other features designed for multiple people planning together?

rankingCriteria.title: Pricing
rankingCriteria.desc: Is the app free or freemium? Are the most useful features locked behind a paywall?

rankingCriteria.title: Ease of use
rankingCriteria.desc: Can a non-technical person set up a trip and invite friends in under five minutes?

rankingCriteria.title: Language support
rankingCriteria.desc: Is the app available in multiple languages, including French?

apps.name: WePlanify
apps.description: WePlanify is the most complete free tool for planning group trips. It combines itinerary building, group polls, shared budget tracking, packing lists, and AI-powered activity discovery in a single collaborative platform. Every feature is free with no hidden paywalls or trial limits.
apps.bestFor: Friend groups wanting everything in one place
strengths: All-in-one: itinerary, polls, budget, packing lists, and activity discovery
Free forever with no feature restrictions
limitations: Mobile app coming soon (currently a responsive web app)

apps.name: Wanderlog
apps.description: Wanderlog is a map-based itinerary builder with route visualization. It lacks group polls, shared budget tracking, and French language support — features that matter most when planning a trip with friends or family.
apps.bestFor: Solo travelers and road trip route planning
strengths: Mobile app available on iOS and Android
Restaurant and attraction suggestions along routes
limitations: No group polls or voting features
No shared budget tracking — only available in English

apps.name: SquadTrip
apps.description: SquadTrip collects payments from trip participants and manages RSVPs. It does not include packing lists, activity discovery, or French support, so groups still need additional tools to cover the full planning process.
apps.bestFor: Trip organizers collecting money from participants
limitations: No packing lists or activity discovery
apps.price: Free with paid features for organizers

apps.name: TripIt
apps.description: TripIt assembles itineraries from forwarded booking emails. It is designed for solo travelers — group collaboration, polls, and shared budgets are not available, and most features require a paid Pro subscription ($49/year).
strengths: Automatic itinerary creation from forwarded emails
limitations: No group collaboration, polls, or shared budgets
Most useful features require Pro subscription ($49/year)

apps.name: Splitwise
apps.description: Splitwise tracks shared expenses across a group. It has no trip planning features — no itinerary, no polls, no packing lists — so most groups need a second app alongside it.
apps.bestFor: Tracking who owes what after expenses
strengths: Expense splitting with complex split options
limitations: Not a trip planner — no itinerary, polls, or packing features
Pro required ($4.99/mo) for receipt scanning and charts

apps.name: Troupe
apps.description: Troupe lets groups vote on activity suggestions using ranked voting. It covers only the decision phase — there is no budget tracking, no packing lists, and no French support, leaving most trip logistics unaddressed.
strengths: Ranked voting system for group decisions
limitations: No budget tracking or packing lists

apps.name: Stippl
apps.tagline: Visual travel planner with community itineraries
apps.description: Stippl is a visual travel planner with community-shared itineraries and photo journals. It lacks group polls, shared budget tracking, and French language support — making it more suited for solo travelers than for coordinating a group trip.
apps.bestFor: Solo travelers who want to document trips visually
limitations: No group polls or voting features
No shared budget tracker — not available in French

apps.name: AvoSquado
apps.description: AvoSquado manages bedroom assignments and bookable activities for large vacation rentals. It covers a narrow niche — there is no itinerary builder, no polls, no budget tracking, and no packing lists.
strengths: Bedroom assignment feature for shared houses
limitations: Narrow focus — not a full trip planner
No itinerary, polls, budget, or packing features

apps.name: Cruzmi
apps.description: Cruzmi is a French mobile app for organizing events and group outings. It offers basic polls and budget tracking but has limited itinerary features, making it more of an event coordination tool than a trip planner.
strengths: Basic polls and shared to-do lists
limitations: Fewer trip planning features compared to dedicated platforms

apps.name: Let's Jetty
apps.description: Let's Jetty provides a visual itinerary builder with a minimal interface. It lacks budget tracking, polls, and packing lists — which means groups planning complex trips will need to supplement it with other tools.
apps.bestFor: Small groups wanting a simple itinerary view
strengths: Quick setup with low learning curve
limitations: No budget tracking, polls, or packing lists
Limited feature set for complex group trips

faqItems.question: What is the best app for planning a group trip?
faqItems.answer: It depends on your needs. WePlanify is the most complete all-in-one option with itinerary planning, polls, budget tracking, and packing lists. Wanderlog excels at map-based itinerary building. SquadTrip is ideal if you need integrated payments. TripIt works best for organizing existing bookings.

faqItems.question: Is there a free app for group travel planning?
faqItems.answer: Yes. WePlanify, Wanderlog, Troupe, and Let's Jetty all offer full-featured free plans. WePlanify stands out by offering all core features — including polls, budget tracking, and packing lists — without any paywall.

faqItems.question: How do you split costs on a group trip?
faqItems.answer: Apps like Splitwise and WePlanify let you log shared expenses and automatically calculate who owes what. Splitwise is dedicated to expense splitting, while WePlanify includes budget tracking alongside itinerary planning and group coordination.

faqItems.question: Can multiple people edit the same trip itinerary?
faqItems.answer: Yes — WePlanify, Wanderlog, and Stippl all support real-time collaborative editing. Everyone in the group can add activities, rearrange days, and leave comments simultaneously.

faqItems.question: What app lets a group vote on travel destinations?
faqItems.answer: WePlanify, SquadTrip, and Cruzmi include built-in polling features where group members can vote on destinations, activities, restaurants, and dates. WePlanify offers unlimited polls with real-time results.

ctaTitle: Ready to plan your next group trip?
ctaText: Stop juggling multiple apps and spreadsheets. WePlanify brings itinerary planning, group polls, budget tracking, packing lists, and AI-powered recommendations into one free platform.
